title: Unfortunately Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Unfortunately Yours" book by Tessa Bailey?
Options:
A. Despite his frustration, he couldn't abandon Natalie if she required his help. Even though it had clearly been hard for her to put aside her self-respect and ask for assistance, he had to be there for her. No, he would always think about it. So he traversed the bothersome street in his hot, constricting suit with his teeth clenched, looking through the crowd for the black-haired goddess he would never get to sleep with but apparently would be marrying, because he had lost his mind. Under the tent it was so hot that he instantly began to sweat. Why did these people insist on gathering to celebrate fermented grape juice? Had none of them heard of baseball? Now that was a reason to assemble outside in the sun.
B. Though annoyed, he couldn't leave Natalie alone if she was in need. Even after she'd swallowed her pride and asked for help when it was clearly difficult, he had to be there. He'd always regret it if he didn't. So he walked across the irritating street in his uncomfortable suit, grinding his teeth, searching the crowd for the dark-haired beauty he lusts for but will apparently marry, because he's lost it. It was sweltering under the tent and he immediately started sweating. Why were these people so insistent on celebrating wine? Had they not discovered baseball? Now that's a reason to get together outside. 
C. Despite his aggravation, he couldn't desert Natalie if she required assistance. Even after she'd overcome her ego and requested help when it was clearly hard, he had to be present. He would always feel guilty if he didn't. So he went across the annoying street in his stuffy suit, clenching his teeth, looking through the crowd for the raven-haired stunner he desires but will seemingly wed, because he's crazy. It was boiling under the tent and he promptly began perspiring. Why were these people so adamant about reveling in wine? Had they never heard of baseball? Now that's a justification to congregate outside.
D. Still, he couldnt walk away from Natalie if she needed him. Not when shed sucked it up and asked for assistance when it clearly had been very difficult to set aside her pride. No, hed dwell on it forever. So he crossed the stupid street in his hot, restrictive suit with his molars grinding together, scanning the crowd for the black-haired goddess he would never get to sleep with but would apparently be marrying, because hed lost his fucking mind. It was so hot under the tent that he immediately started to sweat. Why did these people insist on gathering to celebrate fermented grape juice? Had none of them heard of baseball? Now that was a reason to gather outside in the sun Natalie.
Answer:
